data.title: Milestone 2: Tool broker + 2 read-only tools (egress check, private registry, broker unit tests)

data.body: Review (fresh-context sub-agent): PASS. Verified ff9bd3d live: ruff clean, mypy clean (9 files), pytest 16 passed (~0.4s, no Docker/network). Security boundary traced: broker() is the sole exec path (spec.fn called once after all gates); tool callables underscore-private + __all__=[] + only broker imports them (grep-confirmed); check order matches §6.2 exactly (kind-deny precedes arg-validate so write tools refused even with valid args); egress applied to web_search only, blocks long opaque tokens + sk-/AKIA/JWT/hex patterns + length cap, KB tool exempt; _agent_permitted requires enabled Tool row AND enabled AgentTool link; web_search no-key path returns 'unavailable' before any httpx call, exceptions caught; ApprovalRequired unreachable (no write tool). Test asserts cover every branch. No scope creep. Seed idempotent on live Postgres. Recorded limitations (acceptable MVP backstop): egress bypassable by data fragmentation (TODO'd; -> NAVI-6 backlog), max_cost_per_run=0.0 denies-all footgun (now documented for M3). Applied 2 inline nits. -> done.
